mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.9% is near historical lows, signaling an accommodative monetary stance. Sustained low rates reduce the opportunity cost of holding equities relative to bonds, compressing long-term Treasury yields downward as investors rotate into risk assets. This mechanism operates continuously in low-rate regimes.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day forward window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.4% combined with payroll employment at 146,063 thousand indicates a tight labor market approaching full employment. Wage pressure from labor tightness drives inflation expectations higher, forcing the Federal Reserve to signal future rate increases, which steepens the yield curve and raises 10-year Treasury yields.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.5% AND PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 10 basis points over the 20-day window despite labor market tightness indicators, or the joint signal (UNRATE < 4.5% AND PAYEMS growth > 150k) fails to predict positive yield direction in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 474,948 million dollars and housing starts at 1,215 thousand units signal robust aggregate demand. Strong demand growth increases inflation risk and real economic activity, prompting market expectations of higher future short rates and steeper yield curves, raising 10-year Treasury yields.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% OR HOUST > 1,200 thousand units
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 12 basis points over the 20-day window when either demand trigger is activated, or the signal produces positive yield direction in fewer than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 104.0998 (index 2017=100) reflects supply-side capacity utilization. When industrial production growth accelerates above trend, it signals improving supply conditions that reduce inflation pressure, allowing the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy longer, which depresses long-term Treasury yields.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points over the 60-day window when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.8%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 244.158 (index 1982-1984=100) combined with FEDFUNDS at 0.9% creates a deeply negative real federal funds rate. This regime of financial repression incentivizes investors to demand higher nominal yields on long-duration assets like 10-year Treasuries to compensate for real purchasing power erosion.
candidate_trigger: Real FEDFUNDS rate (FEDFUNDS minus 3-month CPI growth) falls below -1.5%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window when real rates are deeply negative, or the interaction signal fails to predict positive yield direction in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 16,842.4 billion chained 2017 dollars (quarterly) combined with retail sales and housing data indicates strong aggregate demand momentum. Sustained demand growth above trend forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy sooner than markets initially priced, causing a repricing of long-term yields upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 1.0%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 25 basis points over the 60-day window when both growth triggers are activated, or the joint signal produces positive yield direction in fewer than 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.4% is below the estimated natural rate of unemployment (approximately 4.5-4.7%). This sub-natural unemployment regime generates persistent wage and price inflation, which the Federal Reserve must combat through tighter monetary policy, raising equilibrium long-term Treasury yields.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 8 basis points over the 5-day window when UNRATE < 4.3%, or the signal produces positive yield direction in fewer than 48% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 104.0998 combined with payroll employment at 146,063 thousand reveals a supply-constrained economy with limited slack. Supply constraints prevent the economy from absorbing demand without generating inflation, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain higher real rates, which elevates 10-year Treasury yields.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth < 0.3% AND PAYEMS growth > 100k month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 20 basis points over the 60-day window when supply constraints are signaled by the interaction trigger, or the joint signal fails to predict positive yield direction in > 55% of triggered instances.
